name: runCommand
description: Execute a safe, whitelisted cmd.exe command. Allowed: dir, echo, type, npm, yarn, pnpm, bun, python, uv, pip, dotnet.
examples: Execute 'dir' to list the current directory contents
whenToUse: The task is to exec a shell command from the whitelisted set (dir, echo, type, npm, yarn, pnpm, bun, python, uv, pip, dotnet).
The result is a side-effect (build, install, execute) rather than a structured read of a local file.
The command is idempotent or the user explicitly accepts its effect.
whenNotToUse: The task is to read, list, or discover local files. Use readFile / listDirectory / scanDirectory / glob instead.
The task is to modify local files. Use writeFile / editFile instead.
The command is not in the whitelist — this tool will refuse to run it.
